plant_parts.name: root
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa root is a traditional medicinal part used in herbal remedies, primarily for its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. It has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, menstrual cramps, and other conditions related to hormonal imbalance. The root contains compounds like ferulic acid and isofrazidin, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects.

plant_parts.name: rhizome
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa rhizome, which is often used in herbal medicine, contains compounds like cimicifugoside, isofrazidin, and actein. These compounds have been associated with various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The rhizome of Cimicifuga racemosa is traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, such as cramps and hot flashes, due to its potential to modulate hormonal activity.

plant_parts.name: whole plant
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa whole plant is used in herbal medicine, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The root and rhizome of the plant are also used separately, but the whole plant is often used to treat menstrual disorders and menopausal symptoms. The medicinal properties of the whole plant are attributed to the presence of various compounds, including ferulic acid and isoflavones.

plant_parts.name: dried root
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa dried root has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, including hot flashes and night sweats, due to its estrogen-like properties. The dried root of this plant contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including phenolic glycosides and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Studies have suggested that Cimicifuga racemosa dried root may also have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings.

plant_parts.name: root extract
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa root extract has been traditionally used to alleviate menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats, due to its estrogen-like properties. It is also used to treat menstrual cramps, anxiety, and other conditions related to hormonal imbalance. Studies have shown that cimicifuga racemosa root extract may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits.

plant_parts.name: dried rhizome
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa dried rhizome is used in traditional herbal medicine to treat menstrual disorders and menopausal symptoms. The dried rhizome is known to contain compounds such as actein and cimicifugic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. It is often used to relieve symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, menopause, and other gynecological conditions.

plant_parts.name: stem
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa stem is a medicinal part of the black cohosh plant, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The stem of the black cohosh is rich in triterpene glycosides, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. These glycosides are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making Cimicifuga racemosa stem a valuable component in traditional medicine.

plant_parts.name: aerial parts
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa aerial parts are used medicinally due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The aerial parts have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, such as menstrual cramps and PMS symptoms, as well as for menopausal symptoms. Research has also suggested potential benefits of cimicifuga racemosa in reducing hot flashes and improving sleep quality.

plant_parts.name: crude root
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa crude root contains sesquiterpenes, including actein and cimicifugic acid, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. The root is also a rich source of phenolic compounds and lignans, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds may contribute to the root's traditional uses in treating menstrual disorders and other health conditions.

plant_parts.name: rhizome extract
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa rhizome extract has been used traditionally to treat a variety of health conditions, including menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. The rhizome extract is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Research has also suggested that cimicifuga racemosa rhizome extract may have a positive impact on mood and cognitive function in women experiencing menopause.

plant_parts.name: gall
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa gall has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic benefits. The gall, specifically the dried and processed gallbladder, is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is often used to support the health of the digestive system and to alleviate various health issues.

plant_parts.name: leaf
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa leaf has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings. The leaf of this plant is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds and has been studied for its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and effects of Cimicifuga racemosa leaf on human health.

plant_parts.name: dried stem
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa dried stem is commonly used in herbal medicine to treat menstrual and menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats. The dried stem is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, cimicifuga racemosa dried stem has been used to regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome.

plant_parts.name: flower
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa flower is not typically used in herbal medicine, but rather the root of the plant is the medicinal part. The root of Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, is often used to treat symptoms of menopause, including hot flashes and night sweats. It is also used to treat menstrual cramps, anxiety, and other women's health issues.

plant_parts.name: flower extract
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa flower extract is known for its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. It may also exhibit estrogenic activity, which could contribute to its use in menopausal symptom relief. Additionally, the flower extract has been studied for its potential in reducing hot flashes and other symptoms associated with menopause.

plant_parts.name: seed
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa seed is not typically considered a medicinal part of the plant, as the seeds are not commonly used for medicinal purposes. However, the root of Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, is often used to treat symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. The roots contain compounds like actein, which may contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.

plant_parts.name: flower oil
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa flower oil is obtained from the flowers of the black cohosh plant and is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. This oil is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for skin health and pain relief. The oil is also used to treat menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms due to its potential to balance hormonal levels.

plant_parts.name: seed oil
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa seed oil has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This oil may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties may contribute to its potential anti-aging benefits and protection against cell damage.

plant_parts.name: bark
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa bark is a medicinal part traditionally used in herbal remedies, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bark of the plant contains compounds like ferulic acid and cimicifugic acid, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, cimicifuga racemosa bark is often used to treat menstrual disorders, menopausal symptoms, and other gynecological issues.

plant_parts.name: tubers
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tubers are the primary medicinal part of the plant, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues. The tubers contain a range of bioactive compounds, including glycosides, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to their medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic effects, making the tubers a valuable resource for traditional and modern medicine.

plant_parts.name: corms
plant_parts.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa corms, the medicinal parts of the black cohosh plant, contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopause and other gynecological issues. The corms of Cimicifuga racemosa have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and estrogen-modulating properties, making them a popular natural remedy for hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms. Research has shown that extracts from Cimicifuga racemosa corms may also have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects, contributing to their potential therapeutic benefits.
